signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry + Positioning
signals.text: USD/JPY at 156.40 — 300bp Fed-BoJ carry sustains the structural bid, but reports of a 14-point US-Iran MOU trigger a -0.45% DXY tumble and JPY short-covering. JPY COT LF net short -88,543 (30.6% long) remains a crowded carry trade vulnerable to further de-risking. While VIX at 17.2 indicates normal volatility, the sharp -6.85% drop in WTI oil and cooling Middle East tensions reduce the 'inflation-hedge' USD bid. Carry dominates the regime until VIX crosses 25, but intervention risk remains the primary ceiling.

signals.title: NZD/CAD — G8 Bilateral Theme + RBNZ Bias
signals.text: NZD/CAD G8 spread at 1.191% as NZD outperforms peers (+0.614%) while CAD lags (-0.577%). NZD/USD at 0.5959 is supported by a hawkish RBNZ bias (hike priced for 27 May) vs a neutral BoC at 2.25%. NZD COT LF net short -16,833 (23.0% long) vs a hike bias creates a COT-OIS divergence; if RBNZ signals a hike, a short-squeeze in NZD crosses is the primary risk. CAD remains pressured by the -6.85% intraday collapse in WTI oil prices.

signals.title: AUD/USD — RBA Hike + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7240 testing 2022 highs following the RBA's 25bp hike to 4.35%. AUD COT LF net long +48,299 (74.8% long) shows institutional conviction, while retail sentiment is 89% short. This extreme retail-institutional divergence suggests retail is fading the breakout; a continuation higher triggers retail capitulation. AUD/USD carry advantage at +60bp vs Fed supports the trend as long as US 10Y yields hold below 4.50%.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Correlation Break + Fed Stance
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1752 vs EuroStoxx corr=+0.734 (z=+1.88σ) — the pair is decoupling from its historical inverse relationship with EU equities, now moving in lockstep with risk-on sentiment. Fed's Musalem notes inflation is 'meaningfully above target,' but Goolsbee's focus on productivity gains limits USD upside. Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.0% (+175bp) maintains a structural USD carry advantage, keeping EUR/USD upside capped despite the recent 1M +2.10% rally.
